figures.caption: Figure 2: Categorical (denoted CAT): scatter plots of pair-wise marginals for the best models after HPO. On each plot, a point is drawn for a pair-wise level. E.g. the point for SEX=MALE and ICD10=C20, is drawn at x=0.47 and y=0.55, if 47% and 55% are the observed percentages in the real and synthetic datasets respectively. Numerical (denoted NUM): QQ plots of every variable with the real quantiles on x-axis and the synthetic quantiles on the y-axis.
Table 7: Domain violations on EpiCancerGER dataset. Number of distinct level pairs outside of domain and percentage of samples affected in the synthetic data.
Table 4: Main results, best models after HPO. ↓ denotes lower is better. ↑ denotes higher is better. The MAE2 and Hist_IoU2 metrics rank the models. Bold metrics values denotes the best in dataset.
Table 5: HPO improvement. Comparing metrics after HPO to default hyperparameters. ↓ denotes lower is better. ↑ denotes higher is better.
Table 2: Models included in the benchmark.
Table 8: Variables included in EpiCancerGER dataset.
Figure 3: Evaluation metrics over HPO tuning. The y-axis on the left represents MAE2 and Hist_IoU2 on the right. Cross marks locate when the default hyperparameters were outperformed.
Table 9: ICD-10 Chapters considered for the domain violations analysis Sex vs ICD 3-digit.
Figure 5: Comparison of rankings from different metrics.
Figure 4: Evaluation pair-wise scatter plots over the HPO tuning for USCensus1990.
Table 10: Details of hyperparameters ranges. ’optimized’ denotes hyperparameters tuned during HPO. ’stratified’ means different HPO were run (only the bests are presented in the results).
